description_long: This is a traditional barrel-shaped drum with a wooden body and taut animal skin drumheads on both ends, secured by a network of rope lacing. The wood appears weathered and aged, with visible grain and some surface irregularities, giving it a rustic, hand-crafted look. The drum is mounted on a sturdy wooden stand with carved legs, elevating it off the ground for ease of play. The rope lacing is tightly wound in a geometric pattern, providing both structural integrity and decorative detail. The drumheads are light in color and have a slightly textured, natural finish, contrasting with the darker, rougher wood. No visible handles, labels, or markings are present, and the overall condition suggests it has seen moderate use.
description: A rustic, barrel-shaped wooden drum with animal skin heads, rope lacing, and a carved wooden stand. The drum shows signs of age and use, with a natural, handcrafted appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the drum, rope lacing, and the wooden stand beneath.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the circular drumhead and the carved details of the stand.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the rope pattern and wood texture.
description_view_3: Rear end view focusing on the second drumhead and the symmetrical stand base.
